title: cryptocoryne pygmaea
intro: Cryptocoryne pygmaea, also known as the dwarf Cryptocoryne, is a species of aquatic plant in the family Arumaceae. It is native to Sri Lanka and is commonly cultivated in aquariums for its attractive, small size and vibrant green leaves. Cryptocoryne pygmaea has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing stress and promoting relaxation. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through infusion or decoction, and is also used in herbal preparations such as teas and tinctures.
